fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp=False and skips it when clamp=True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, the clamping block should execute; the condition `not clamp` is inverted so clamping runs when clamp=False instead.
The then-branch of the if-not-clamp block should only execute when clamp is False (i.e., no clamping desired), but the condition is inverted.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (min(y0, y1) <= y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, the final y value must be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; the bug causes this invariant to be violated.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: Before returning, if clamp=True the result must be bounded by the segment endpoints.
